description: Brake control shall maintain commanded braking force within plus or minus 5 percent under normal braking conditions.
Brake control shall detect brake pressure sensor failure within 100 ms of signal loss.
Brake control shall enter degraded braking mode when hydraulic pressure is below the minimum safe threshold.
Brake control shall alert the driver when regenerative braking is unavailable.
Brake control shall prioritize friction braking when regenerative braking torque is insufficient.
Brake control shall prevent unintended brake release during emergency braking.
Brake control shall maintain stable brake force distribution during split-mu road conditions.
Brake control shall record a diagnostic trouble code when brake actuator response exceeds the allowed delay.
Brake control shall disable automatic hold when vehicle speed exceeds the configured release threshold.
Brake control shall confirm brake pedal plausibility using redundant pedal position signals.
Steering assist shall transition to manual fallback within 100 ms after steering angle sensor fault detection.
Steering assist shall limit assistance torque when steering torque sensor values are inconsistent.
Steering assist shall provide driver warning when electric power steering enters degraded mode.
Steering assist shall prevent unintended steering torque during ignition-on self-test.
Steering assist shall store a diagnostic trouble code when motor current exceeds the safe operating limit.
Steering assist shall reduce assistance smoothly when battery voltage falls below the operating threshold.
Steering assist shall verify steering angle sensor plausibility before enabling lane keeping support.
Steering assist shall maintain manual steering availability after power assist failure.
Steering assist shall suppress assistance output when calibration data is invalid.
Steering assist shall detect steering motor overheating and request torque derating.
Headlamp control shall maintain legally compliant beam intensity during supply voltage fluctuation.
Headlamp control shall switch to low beam when high beam actuator feedback is invalid.
Headlamp control shall alert the driver when adaptive headlamp leveling fails.
Headlamp control shall prevent glare by lowering beam angle when rear axle load increases.
Headlamp control shall enter safe default mode if ambient light sensor input is unavailable.
Headlamp control shall verify left and right lamp synchronization during automatic beam adjustment.
Headlamp control shall record a diagnostic trouble code when LED driver temperature exceeds threshold.
Headlamp control shall maintain daytime running lamp operation after non-critical adaptive lighting failure.
Headlamp control shall detect open circuit failure in the low beam output channel.
Headlamp control shall disable adaptive bending function if steering angle input is implausible.
Engine control shall limit torque to prevent wheel spin on icy or low-traction road surfaces.
Engine control shall reduce torque output when accelerator pedal signals are inconsistent.
Engine control shall enter limp-home mode when throttle actuator feedback is invalid.
Engine control shall prevent unintended acceleration during startup initialization.
Engine control shall monitor throttle position response time and flag delayed actuator movement.
Engine control shall suppress torque request when brake override is active.
Engine control shall reduce torque when engine coolant temperature exceeds the thermal protection threshold.
Engine control shall store freeze-frame data after safety-relevant torque monitoring fault detection.
Engine control shall validate torque request plausibility before forwarding output to the powertrain actuator.
Engine control shall reset torque command to safe default after watchdog timeout.
Airbag control shall deploy the frontal airbag within 20 ms after confirmed crash detection.
Airbag control shall suppress passenger airbag deployment when occupant weight is below 30 kg.
Airbag control shall verify crash sensor plausibility before deployment decision.
Airbag control shall record deployment event data after crash-triggered activation.
Airbag control shall alert the driver if the restraint control module detects internal memory corruption.
Airbag control shall disable deployment output if squib circuit resistance is outside the allowed range.
Airbag control shall execute power-on self-test before enabling deployment readiness.
Airbag control shall maintain deployment readiness during transient battery voltage drop.
Airbag control shall detect side-impact sensor failure and set the corresponding diagnostic trouble code.
Airbag control shall prevent unintended deployment during normal driving vibration events.
Instrument cluster shall display coolant temperature within plus or minus 3 degrees Celsius accuracy.
Instrument cluster shall show brake system warning within 1 second after receiving a critical brake fault signal.
Instrument cluster shall display airbag warning when restraint system status is degraded.
Instrument cluster shall show battery thermal warning when battery management system requests driver alert.
Instrument cluster shall display steering assist warning when electric power steering enters fallback mode.
Instrument cluster shall prioritize safety-critical warnings over infotainment notifications.
Instrument cluster shall maintain warning indicator visibility under day and night brightness settings.
Instrument cluster shall detect display communication timeout and enter safe warning display mode.
Instrument cluster shall store the last active warning state after ignition cycle reset.
Instrument cluster shall confirm telltale lamp operation during startup self-check.
Door lock control shall not unlock doors when vehicle speed is above 5 kilometers per hour.
Door lock control shall automatically unlock doors after crash event confirmation.
Door lock control shall ignore unlock requests when child lock safety mode is active.
Door lock control shall detect door latch sensor inconsistency and notify the driver.
Door lock control shall maintain locked state after transient communication loss with body control module.
Door lock control shall record a diagnostic trouble code when lock actuator movement is incomplete.
Door lock control shall verify driver door closed status before enabling drive-away locking.
Door lock control shall prevent unintended unlocking during remote key authentication failure.
Door lock control shall disable passive unlock when key signal strength is below the security threshold.
Door lock control shall provide audible feedback after successful remote lock command.
Battery management system shall alert the driver when battery temperature exceeds safe thermal thresholds.
Battery management system shall limit charging current when cell temperature exceeds the warning threshold.
Battery management system shall open the high-voltage contactor when isolation resistance is below safe threshold.
Battery management system shall detect cell voltage imbalance above the configured diagnostic threshold.
Battery management system shall reduce discharge power when battery state of charge is critically low.
Battery management system shall store fault data when thermal runaway risk condition is detected.
Battery management system shall verify current sensor plausibility using redundant measurement paths.
Battery management system shall request vehicle shutdown when high-voltage interlock loop is open.
Battery management system shall communicate charging limitation status to the instrument cluster.
Battery management system shall enter safe state after internal watchdog failure.
Cruise control shall disengage immediately when the brake pedal is pressed.
Cruise control shall disengage when accelerator pedal position exceeds override threshold.
Cruise control shall not activate below the minimum configured vehicle speed.
Cruise control shall alert the driver when radar input required for adaptive cruise is unavailable.
Cruise control shall maintain target speed within plus or minus 2 kilometers per hour on level road.
Cruise control shall reject activation request when brake switch status is invalid.
Cruise control shall reduce set speed smoothly when following distance becomes unsafe.
Cruise control shall cancel control output after communication timeout with powertrain controller.
Cruise control shall store diagnostic data when speed control actuator response is delayed.
Cruise control shall provide visual indication when the system is active.
Rearview camera shall display the camera feed within 2 seconds after reverse gear selection.
Rearview camera shall show a driver warning when camera video signal is unavailable.
Rearview camera shall maintain guide line overlay alignment within the configured tolerance.
Rearview camera shall switch off the rear display after reverse gear is disengaged.
Rearview camera shall detect camera communication timeout and display fallback warning screen.
Rearview camera shall maintain image brightness adjustment during low-light reverse operation.
Rearview camera shall prevent frozen image display for more than 500 ms during active reverse mode.
Rearview camera shall verify video frame freshness before rendering the image to the display.
Rearview camera shall store diagnostic trouble code when camera module self-test fails.
Rearview camera shall disable dynamic guide lines if steering angle input is invalid.
